questions.question: Describe the three main components of a file system structure. Explain their purpose in the context of file system implementation.
questions.hint: File system components include Inode, Directory, and File.

questions.question: A file system consists of two types of blocks: data blocks and inode blocks. Explain the difference between them and their storage locations in a file system.
questions.hint: Data blocks store actual file data, while inode blocks store file metadata.

questions.question: Sketch the structure of a file system, including the components mentioned in the lecture, and explain how they interact with each other.
questions.hint: Include the file system hierarchy, inode structure, and how data is stored in the file system.
